metadata.notes: EBU Level 4 system. Brown/Red sticker. Precision-style 1!c (may be short), strong 1!d, Level 4 majors, multi-meaning 2-level openings.

meaning.description: One of: (a) balanced 4333/4432/5332 (possibly 2254); (b) 10-15 unbalanced, no 5-card major, at most one 4-card major. Announced 'May be short'.
16+, not game forcing. Alert. Balanced/semi-balanced 16-19 HCP; two-suiter or three-suiter 16-GF; single-suiter 16-GF (less than 3-loser hand).
10-15 points, unbalanced, 4+ !h. May have a longer minor (canape). Alert.
10-15 points, unbalanced, 5+ !s. May have a longer minor (canape). Alert.
Balanced (4333/4432/5332). Range varies by seat and vulnerability — announced with the range.
Direct seat double: modified suction escape. For 9-15 range, opener may pass with 13-15 (partner XX) or bid a suit with 9-12.
Protective double — Aardvark with Halmic Redouble
One of: weak !d (6+, 5-9); weak !h+!s 5-4+ (5-9); 20-23 balanced; GF !h+!s 5-4+ (3 losers). Alert.
One of: weak !h (6+, 5-9); weak !s+!c 5-4+ (5-9); GF !s+!c 5-4+ (3 losers). Alert.
One of: weak !s (6+, 5-9); weak !c+!d 5-4+ (5-9); GF !c+!d 5-4+ (3 losers). Alert.
One of: weak !c (7+, 5-9); weak !h+minor 5-4+ (5-9); GF !h+minor 5-4+ (3 losers). Alert.
One of: weak !s+!d 5-4+ (5-9); GF !s+!d 5-4+ (3 losers); 24+ balanced. Alert.
GF !c (3-loser) OR 7-card preempt in !d. Typically 7 cards, occasionally good 6 or bad 8. Alert.
GF !d (3-loser) OR 7-card preempt in !h. Typically 7 cards, occasionally good 6 or bad 8. Alert.
GF !h (3-loser) OR 7-card preempt in !s. Typically 7 cards, occasionally good 6 or bad 8. Alert.
GF !s (3-loser) OR Gambling 3NT (solid 7-8 card minor, ≤Q outside: AKQJxxx / AKQxxxx / AKxxxxxx). Alert.
4-level preempt in either minor (usually 8 cards). Alert.
Specific aces ask — missing at most 2 aces or void. Happy playing 6-level opposite some responses, 7-level opposite others. Not alerted.
11 tricks missing 2 of top 3 trump honours (!a, !k, !q). Raise one level per trump honour held by partner.
11 tricks missing 2 of top 3 trump honours (!a, !k, !q). Raise one level per trump honour held by partner.
Takeout double — standard takeout or strong hand. After double and partner's response, cue by doubler = general force (not suit-agreeing).
Direct 1NT overcall: 15-17 balanced with stop (may contain singleton Ace). 1NT continuations apply. After (1x)-(1N)-(2y): cue of 1x = Stayman without stop; other bids = Lebensohl.

meaningOverride.description: Protective 1NT: 11-14 balanced with stop. Same continuations apply.

meaning.description: Simple suit overcall — constructive, natural 5+ cards, 10+ HCP. Responsive cue by partner = good raise (constructive+). Direct raises = preemptive.
Opponent raises / cue-bids. Responsive double by partner = penalty interest or good raise.
Opponent doubles — penalty or takeout interest.
Jump overcall — weak, 6-card suit, 4+ HCP favourable up to simple-overcall strength. Protective seat = intermediate 11-15, reasonable 6+ card suit. Also applies after (P)-(P)-(1NT).
Jump cuebid of opponent's suit — stopper-asking for 3NT; promises a long running side suit.
3NT overcall — long running suit with stop; gambling style. Responses as for gambling 3NT opening.
Michaels cuebid over a minor opening — 5-5+ in both majors (!h and !s). Weak or strong. Bidding a shown suit = pass-or-correct.
Michaels cuebid over a major opening — 5-5+ in the other major and either minor. Weak or strong. Bidding a shown suit = pass-or-correct. 2NT = invitational+ enquiry for the minor.
Unusual 2NT — over a major: 5-5+ minors; over a minor: other minor + either major. Weak or strong. Bidding a shown suit = pass-or-correct.

keri.name: Keri (5-card puppet)

meaning.description: Keri — puppet to 2!d; various hand types
Weak hand with diamonds — to play 2!d
4+ !s; may also have 4 !h. Opener now bids naturally.
Opener has 5-card !h — shows instead of relaying
Forcing inquiry — fit or other hand
Opener has 5-card !s — shows instead of relaying

keri.description: 2!c over 1NT = 5-card puppet Keri. Announced 'Keri'. Covers weak hands wanting clubs/diamonds, and any hand wanting to find a 5-3 major fit.

rkcb.name: Roman Key Card Blackwood 1430

meaning.description: Roman Key Card Blackwood — asks for key cards (4 aces + K!{suit})
Queen ask — do you have Q!{suit}?
Q!{suit} + K!s (only if !s not agreed suit)
Queen ask — do you have Q!{suit}?

rkcb.description: 4NT asks for key cards (4 aces + K of agreed suit). 1430 responses.

rkqg.name: Roman Key-Quant Gerber

meaning.description: Roman Key-Quant Gerber — asks aces, allows quantitative slam invite
Accept quantitative — small slam in NT
Accept quantitative — small slam in NT

rkqg.description: 4!c over NT asks for aces with quantitative follow-up option.

1c-resp.name: 1!c Responses (1st/2nd seat)

meaning.description: 12-15 unbalanced, exactly 4!h (4441 or longer minor canape)
12-15 unbalanced, exactly 4!s (4441 or longer minor canape)
Balanced, no game interest — natural non-forcing weak takeout

1c-resp.description: Responses to 1!c (12-15 balanced or 10-15 unbalanced no 5-card major ≤1 four-card major). 1!d=0-8 relay; 1!h=9-15 any; 1!s=15+ GF; 1NT/2x=transfer weak jump shifts.

1d-resp.name: 1!d Responses (suction relays)

meaning.description: Natural — 5+!s (may have longer minor, canape possible)
8+ game forcing relay — asks opener to describe via suction rebids
Suction: !d single-suiter OR !h+!s two-suiter OR !d+!h+!s three-suiter (16-21)
Single-suited !d — relay further to ask HCP range
Agree !h — slam try; Swiss/RKCB to follow
Agree !s — slam try; Swiss/RKCB to follow
Suction: !h single-suiter OR !s+!c two-suiter OR !h+!s+!c three-suiter (16-21)
Suction: !s single-suiter OR !c+!d two-suiter OR !s+!c+!d three-suiter (16-21)
Suction: !c single-suiter OR !h+!d two-suiter OR !c+!h+!d three-suiter (16-21)
Relay — asks opener to clarify (3!c = further enquiry)

1d-resp.description: Responses to 1!d (16+, alert, not necessarily GF). 1!h=0-7 relay; 1!s=8+ GF with suction relay rebids; 1NT/2x=transfer weak jump shifts.

2c-resp.name: 2!c Responses

meaning.description: Pass or correct — prefers !d; opener passes with weak !d, rebids otherwise
Non-forcing — major focused; pass if min weak majors (!h preference)
Non-forcing — major focused; pass if min weak majors (!s preference)
Forcing 1 round — how good are your diamonds?

2c-resp.description: Responses to 2!c multi — weak !d (6+, 5-9) OR weak !h+!s 5-4+ (5-9) OR 20-23 balanced OR GF !h+!s 5-4+ (3 losers).

2d-resp.name: 2!d Responses
2d-resp.description: Responses to 2!d multi — weak !h (6+, 5-9) OR weak !s+!c 5-4+ (5-9) OR GF !s+!c 5-4+ (3 losers).

2h-resp.name: 2!h Responses
2h-resp.description: Responses to 2!h multi — weak !s (6+, 5-9) OR weak !c+!d 5-4+ (5-9) OR GF !c+!d 5-4+ (3 losers).

2s-resp.name: 2!s Responses
2s-resp.description: Responses to 2!s multi — weak !c (7+, 5-9) OR weak !h+minor 5-4+ (5-9) OR GF !h+minor 5-4+ (3 losers).

3s-resp.name: Responses to 3♠ Opening

meaning.description: Pass or correct — gambling: pass=!c; opener bids 4!d=!d
Singleton ask (GF !s). Opener: 4!h=singleton !h; 4!s=singleton !s; 4NT=no singletons; 5!c/5!d=singleton in other minor
Pass or correct at 5-level — gambling: pass=!c; 5!d=!d

3s-resp.description: Responses to 3!s (GF !s 3-loser OR gambling 3NT with solid 7-8 card minor, ≤Q outside). 3NT=stops in 3 suits; clubs at any level=pass-or-correct; 4!d=singleton ask.

1nt-resp.name: 1NT Continuations

meaning.description: 4-card !h support, maximum, no side 4-card suit — super-accept
4-card !s support, maximum, no side 4-card suit — super-accept
Range enquiry OR inv+ transfer to !c (ambiguous until rebid)
Minimum 1NT — opener sign-off or invite declined
Any maximum (pass if 2!s showed clubs; continue otherwise)
Long suit needing help to run (slam try or 3NT probe) — opener passes with Kxx+ or bids 3NT
To play 3!c — no fit, no cover card
Cover card in !c — good to play 3NT
Slam try in !c — asks opener for help
Small doubleton in !c — not suitable for !c slam
Slam try in !d — asks opener for help
Small doubleton in !d — not suitable for !d slam

1nt-resp.description: Continuations after any natural 1NT opening. 2!c=Keri (separate ref). Transfers to !h/!s/!d, 2!s range/!c, minor slam tries, 5-5 majors.

2nt-resp.name: 2NT Continuations

meaning.description: 5-card puppet Stayman — asks for 5-card major or 4-card major
To play 3NT (no !h fit, or prefers NT)
Puppet to 3NT — either to play or slam try in a minor
5!s + 4!h — to play in 3NT or explore game

2nt-resp.description: Continuations after any natural 2NT (20-23 balanced, or as opener's rebid). 5-card puppet Stayman, transfers, minor preference, RKQG, Viscount.

viscount.name: Viscount

meaning.description: Viscount — bid 4-card suits up the line (like Baron); slam investigation in NT
4+ !s, no 4-card suit below (flat hand, 4333 or similar)

viscount.description: 4NT over NT = bid 4-card suits up the line (Baron-style). Alert.

lebensohl.name: Lebensohl (over 1NT overcalled by any suit)

meaning.description: Puppet to 3!c — allows slower (invite or stop-showing) sequences
Natural, invitational (any suit below the overcalled suit — schematic)
Cue of overcalled suit (schematic) — Staymanic WITH stop
To play 3NT — promises stop in overcalled suit
Direct cue of overcalled suit (schematic — replace strain with actual overcalled suit) — Staymanic, DENIES stop
To play 3NT — DENIES stop in overcalled suit

lebensohl.description: After our 1NT overcalled by any natural suit (x): 2-level bids NF; 3-level GF natural; direct cue of overcalled suit=Staymanic no stop; 3NT=no stop; 2NT puppet to 3!c for slower sequences (suit below cue=invite; cue via 2NT=Staymanic with stop; 3NT via 2NT=with stop). X=penalties. Note: cue bid nodes below are schematic — replace strain with the actual overcalled suit.

1h-l4-resp.name: 1!h (Level 4) Responses

meaning.description: Good raise to 3!h or slam try in !h — forces to game or invites slam
Extras but no suitable trial or cue — to play
Fit jump — good raise + good side !s suit
Fit jump — good raise + good side !c suit
Fit jump — good raise + good side !d suit
Good raise to 4!h — 4-card support, balanced values

1h-l4-resp.description: Responses to 1!h L4 — 10-15 unbalanced, 4+ !h, may have longer minor (canape). Alert bid.

1s-l4-resp.name: 1!s (Level 4) Responses

meaning.description: Good raise to 3!s or slam try in !s — forces to game or invites slam
Extras but no suitable trial or cue — to play
Fit jump — good raise + good side !c suit
Fit jump — good raise + good side !d suit
Fit jump — good raise + good side !h suit
Good raise to 4!s — 4-card support, balanced values

1s-l4-resp.description: Responses to 1!s L4 — 10-15 unbalanced, 5+ !s, may have longer minor (canape). Alert bid.

3xfer-resp.name: 3!c/3!d/3!h Transfer Responses

meaning.description: Complete transfer — to play vs weak option; no extras
Shortage ask (one step above completing transfer) — are you weak or GF? If weak: 3NT=no shortage; 4x=shortage in that suit; If GF: GF responses
To play 3NT (vs weak option only; GF explores further)
General Swiss for weak option in transfer suit
General Swiss for weak option in transfer suit

3xfer-resp.description: Responses to 3!c (GF !c or preempt !d), 3!d (GF !d or preempt !h), 3!h (GF !h or preempt !s). Complete transfer = to play vs weak; step above = shortage ask; 3NT vs weak = to play; 4!c/4!d = Swiss for weak option.

1nt-doubled.name: 1NT Doubled Escape

meaning.description: Forces partner to XX. Either: strong hand (to play 1NTXx) OR weak with !c and another suit.
Forced. Either to play 1NTXx (strong) OR relaying for partner's suit.
Bid 4-card suits up the line (distributional escape)
Forces partner to bid 2!c. Weak with !c OR !d+!h.
Weak with majors (!h+!s) — pass or correct

1nt-doubled.description: Modified suction after 1NT-(X). Pass forces partner's XX; XX forces partner's 2!c (pass=!c, 2!d=!d+!h); direct 2!c puppets partner's 2!d (pass=!d+!s, 2!h=majors); 2!d=!d+!s; 2!h=!h; 2!s=!s.

meaning.description: Complete transfer to 4!h — to play (after 4!c opening)
Complete transfer to 4!s — to play (after 4!d opening)
Slam try with support (after 4!c): opener completes = min; cue bids = max with cue
Slam try with support (after 4!d): opener completes = min; cue bids = max with cue

3nt-open-resp.name: Responses to 3NT Opening

meaning.description: Pass or correct — pass=!c; opener bids 4!d=!d

3nt-open-resp.description: Responses to 3NT opening (4-level preempt in either minor, 8 cards). Clubs at any level=pass-or-correct (pass=!c, diamonds=!d); 4!d=enquiry (4!h=!c, 4!s=!d).

4nt-aces-resp.name: 4NT Specific Aces Responses
4nt-aces-resp.description: Responses to 4NT opening — specific ace ask. Opener missing ≤2 aces or void.

general-swiss.name: General Swiss

meaning.description: Sign off — at most 4 CPs combined, no slam interest
Exactly 8 CPs — sign off in small slam
Sign off — at most 3 CPs combined, no slam interest
Exactly 7 CPs — sign off small slam

general-swiss.description: 4!c/4!d control-point slam try once GF established and suit agreed. A=2, K/singleton/Q-of-trumps=1. 11 CP = small slam, 13 = grand. Adjust: +2 if GF shown by both, -2 if one hand was weak (weak opening or 0-7 response to 1!d).

michaels-resp.name: Michaels Cue Responses

meaning.description: Pass or correct — preference for lower shown suit at lowest level (schematic). Overcaller: pass=weak, correct to next suit=weak with the other suit, break to new suit=strong.
Pass or correct — preference for higher shown suit (schematic). Overcaller: pass=weak, correct to lower suit=weak with lower suit, break=strong.
Invitational+ enquiry for overcaller's other suit and strength
Re-cue = strong raise agreeing advancer's preferred suit (schematic)
Invitational+ with lower shown suit (non-pass-or-correct context). Overcaller: min-weak=3-level game; good-weak=game; strong=Swiss.
Invitational+ with higher shown suit (non-pass-or-correct context). Overcaller: min-weak=raise; good-weak=game; strong=Swiss.

michaels-resp.description: After our Michaels cue (over minor=both majors 5-5+; over major=other major + either minor 5-5+). Weak or strong. Bidding a shown suit at any level=pass-or-correct; overcaller corrects to next suit=weak, breaks to another suit=strong. Non-shown suits: re-cue=strong raise of preferred suit; other non-shown suit=strong with remaining option; 2NT (if available)=invitational+ enquiry; 4!c/4!d=Swiss for known major; 3NT=to play.

2nt-multi-resp.name: 2NT (multi) Responses

meaning.description: Simple preference — pass or rebid for weak two-suiter
Preemptive raise !d (any rebid by opener = GF)
Preemptive raise !s (any rebid by opener = GF)
Transfer preference invitational for !h (opener breaks if GF two-suiter)
Transfer preference invitational for !s (opener breaks if GF two-suiter)
Transfer preference mixed raise — !h side interest
Transfer preference mixed raise — !s side interest

2nt-multi-resp.description: Responses to 2NT opening — weak !s+!d 5-4+ (5-9) OR GF !s+!d 5-4+ (3 losers) OR 24+ balanced.

aardvark-halmic.name: Aardvark with Halmic Redouble
aardvark-halmic.description: After 1NT-(P)-P-(X). Suit bids show a two-suiter in that suit plus any higher suit. Redouble (Halmic) forces 2!c — weak single-suiter; partner passes with !c or corrects to their suit.

unusual2nt-resp.name: Unusual 2NT Responses

meaning.description: Pass or correct to lower shown suit. Overcaller: pass=weak, correct to next=weak with other suit, break=strong.
Pass or correct to higher shown suit. Overcaller: pass=weak, correct=weak with lower suit, break=strong.
Invitational with lower shown suit (when suits known). Overcaller: min-weak=raise; good-weak=game; strong=Swiss or other.
Invitational with higher shown suit (when suits known). Overcaller: min-weak=raise; good-weak=game; strong=other.
Re-cue = any stronger hand; overcaller's responses show which other suit (when suits not yet specified)

unusual2nt-resp.description: After our 2NT unusual (over major=both minors 5-5+; over minor=other minor + either major 5-5+). Weak or strong. Bidding a shown suit at any level=pass-or-correct; overcaller corrects=weak, breaks=strong. Non-shown suits: re-cue=any stronger hand (responses = which other suit); 3NT=to play.
